title: trifolium variegatum f. major
intro: Trifolium variegatum f. major, also known as the Large-flowered Clover or White Trifolium, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family, characterized by its white flowers with red spots and distinctive leaf shape. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Trifolium variegatum f. major include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
